subject: DB2 Common Server
object_value: client-server version of DB2/MVS RDBMS running on RS/6000 (AIX) and OS/2 Warp/Warp Connect
verbatim_quote: DB2 common server (also called 'DB2') is a client-server version of IBM's DB2/MVS RDBMS that runs on IBM's RS/6000 workstations over IBM's AIX version of Unix and on PCs running OS/2 Warp and Warp Connect.

subject: DB2 Common Server
object_value: HP-UX and Sun Solaris; porting in progress to Siemens Nixdorf, Windows NT, and PowerPC
verbatim_quote: IBM has recently ported DB2 to the HP and Sun Unix platforms and is presently porting it to the Siemens Nixdorf, Windows NT, and PowerPC platforms

subject: DB2 Common Server
object_value: major player and strong competitor in the DORS market, especially in the IBM-workstation-server market
verbatim_quote: DB2 is a major player and strong competitor in the DORS market, especially in the IBM-workstation-server market.

subject: DB2 solution
object_value: core DB2 RDBMS plus DataPropagator, DataRefresher, DataJoiner, DataHub, Visualizer, DB2 Parallel Edition
verbatim_quote: The DB2 solution includes the core DB2 RDBMS plus related products that add value for particular user needs, such as DataPropagator and DataRefresher for mass data loads and change-data replication, DataJoiner for distributed and heterogeneous data SQL joins

subject: DB2
object_value: databases requiring connectivity to other IBM platforms; middle tier of three-tier solutions; medium-to-large-scale data-warehousing; mission-critical OLTP
verbatim_quote: IS buyers should consider DB2 especially for databases requiring connectivity to other IBM platforms, where IBM services are an important consideration...and as the middle tier of a three-tier solution requiring a mainframe or AS/400 minicomputer as the top tier.

subject: DB2 v2
object_value: has added functionality to compete on equal technological basis with, and in some cases surpass, other major DORS suppliers
verbatim_quote: DB2 common server's Version 2 has added the functionality necessary for DB2 to compete on an equal technological basis with-and in some cases surpass-the other major distributed, open RDBMS suppliers.

subject: Aberdeen Group
object_value: IS buyers should take a new and closer look at DB2 for distributed, open production systems
verbatim_quote: Aberdeen believes that it is time for IS buyers to take a new and closer look at DB2 for distributed, open production systems.

subject: DB2 v2
object_value: OS/2, AIX, Windows NT servers; beta on HP-UX, Solaris, Sinix; DB2 Web server support upcoming
verbatim_quote: DB2 version 2 is available on OS/2, AIX, and Windows NT servers, and in beta on HP's HP-UX, Sun's Solaris, and Siemens-Nixdorf's Sinix. Support for a DB2 Web server is upcoming.

subject: DB2
object_value: TCP/IP, SPX/IPX, NetBIOS, SNA LU6.2 (APPC/APPN); interconnection to MVS, OS/400, VSE, VM via DRDA
verbatim_quote: DB2 supports the TCP/IP, SPX/IPX, NetBIOS, and SNA LU6.2 (APPC/APPN) protocols, and allows interconnection to the MVS, OS/400, VSE, and VM IBM platforms via DRDA

subject: DRDA
object_value: eleven other suppliers besides IBM have implemented DRDA access to DB2 data
verbatim_quote: Eleven other suppliers have also implemented DRDA access to DB2 data.

subject: IBM
object_value: vast global direct-sales force, often bundled with IBM hardware and services; channel-enabling for version 2
verbatim_quote: IBM sells DB2 primarily through a vast global direct-sales force, often bundled with IBM hardware and services.

subject: DB2/MVS
object_value: over 10 years of datacenter and mission-critical application experience on the mainframe
verbatim_quote: DB2/MVS on the mainframe has over 10 years of datacenter and mission-critical-application experience, giving DB2 common server added robustness and OLTP scalability in many situations.

subject: DB2 on RS/6000 J30
object_value: 3119.16 tpmC at $349/tpmC on an 8-way RS/6000 model J30
verbatim_quote: a recent TPC-C result on an 8-way RS/6000 model J30 shows DB2 delivering 3119.16 tpmC at 349 $/tpmC.

subject: DB2 on RS/6000 J30
object_value: $349 per tpmC on an 8-way RS/6000 model J30
verbatim_quote: a recent TPC-C result on an 8-way RS/6000 model J30 shows DB2 delivering 3119.16 tpmC at 349 $/tpmC.

subject: DB2 v2
object_value: multithreading support for SMP scalability matching other major DORS suppliers
verbatim_quote: version 2 is now 'SMP-enabled', matching other major DORS suppliers in OLTP-type parallel performance features.

subject: DB2 cost-based optimizer
object_value: query rewrite, more alternatives, better non-uniform data distribution detection, pre-fetching, parallel I/O, asynchronous writes
verbatim_quote: A major performance enhancer in version 2 is a reworking of IBM's cost-based optimizer to perform more in-depth analysis of queries, including 'query rewrite' of complex queries

subject: DB2 Parallel Edition
object_value: loosely-coupled RS/6000s with HACMP or LAN, and IBM RS/6000 SP MPP systems; parallelizes joins, inserts, updates, deletes, index, backup
verbatim_quote: DB2 Parallel Edition is available on loosely-coupled RS/6000s with HACMP or connected via a LAN, and on IBM's high-end RS/6000 SP Massively-Parallel Processing (MPP) systems.

subject: DB2
object_value: parallel backup/restore on tablespace basis; integration with ADSM for mainframe and AS/400 backup
verbatim_quote: DB2 version 2 supports parallel backup and restore on a tablespace basis. This allows high performance in a key operation that has proven a major gating factor in scaling DORS databases to the terabyte range.

subject: DataPropagator
object_value: capture-stage to staging area then apply-stage to destination; log-based; minimal overhead; operates in virtual isolation
verbatim_quote: DataPropagator first provides a 'capture' stage to a staging area, then an 'apply' stage to the destination database...DataPropagator's implementation allows replication to each RDBMS copy to occur in parallel with normal RDBMS processing, with minimal overhead.

subject: DataPropagator
object_value: supports time-interval-based replication (e.g., hourly or daily mass refresh) across DRDA-supporting DBMSs
verbatim_quote: DataPropagator allows time-interval-based replication (e.g., it can propagate changes every hour or send a mass 'refresh' change once a day) across DBMSs supporting IBM's DRDA protocol.

subject: DataPropagator
object_value: hotspot reduction, load balancing, fan-out, update batching, push-pull for mobile, data compression before transfer
verbatim_quote: DataPropagator also offers communications-performance improvement via 'hotspot reduction'...load balancing, sending of replications in sets of messages rather than singly, data 'fan-out'...and update batching.

subject: DataPropagator
object_value: upcoming version will deliver update-anywhere robustness to ensure global data consistency
verbatim_quote: An upcoming version of DataPropagator will deliver added update-anywhere robustness by providing features that take unusual care to ensure global data consistency when multiple updates are being performed simultaneously.

subject: DataJoiner
object_value: supports distributed joins across relational, IMS, and VSAM; cost-based global optimizer considers networks and I/O speed; provides location/network transparency
verbatim_quote: DataJoiner provides transparent SQL-based data access, not only across DB2 instances, but also across dissimilar data management systems such as IMS.

subject: DataJoiner
object_value: Visualizer, Lotus Approach, Microsoft Access; IBM VisualAge; Sybase/Powersoft PowerBuilder
verbatim_quote: End users can invoke DataJoiner from Visualizer, Lotus Approach, and Microsoft Access, and programmers from IBM's VisualAge and Sybase/Powersoft's PowerBuilder.

subject: DB2
object_value: two-phase commit across LAN internetworks and IBM hardware including RS/6000, PCs, AS/400 (OS/400), mainframes (MVS) via CICS, Encina, or Tuxedo TP monitors
verbatim_quote: DB2 provides full 2PC support across LAN internetworks and across IBM hardware in the enterprise, including replication between RS/6000s, PCs, AS/400 minicomputers...

subject: DB2 SQL
object_value: 1992 ANSI SQL Entry level, plus some Intermediate/Full functions and proposed SQL3 standard
verbatim_quote: DB2's SQL presently follows almost all of the the 1992 ANSI standard (Entry), plus some functions in the Intermediate and Full standard and in the proposed SQL3 standard.

subject: DRDA gateways
object_value: Informix, Oracle, and Sybase support client-side remote access via DRDA Application Requester
verbatim_quote: At present, Informix, Oracle, and Sybase among DORS suppliers support client-side remote access via DRDA (Application Requester, Remote Unit of Work).

subject: DB2
object_value: CICS, Transarc Encina, Novell Tuxedo; implements X/Open XA API
verbatim_quote: This includes long-standing support for IBM's widely-popular CICS TP monitor, plus support for Transarc's Encina and Novell's Tuxedo and implementation of the X/Open XA API.

subject: DB2
object_value: IBM RS/6000 HACMP clustering with failover; backup/recovery field-proven over a decade
verbatim_quote: DB2 supports the IBM RS/6000's HACMP clustering solution, which includes extensive failover features. DB2's other high-availability features, such as its backup and recovery, have been field-proven over a decade.

subject: DB2 v2
object_value: DB2 Performance Monitor, Visual Explain, SNMP subagent; database MIB support
verbatim_quote: Database management tools arriving with DB2 version 2-especially DB2 Performance Monitor, Visual Explain, and the SNMP subagent-provide extensive database statistics, monitoring, and tuning capabilities.

subject: DB2
object_value: stored procedures, triggers (before/after insert/delete/update), column/table constraints, user-defined functions and data types
verbatim_quote: DB2 provides extensive business-rules support, such as stored procedures, triggers (including triggers before and after inserts, deletes, and updates), column and table constraints, and user-defined functions and data types.

subject: DB2
object_value: character and double-byte character (internationalization); large data types up to 2 GB; CD-ROM ingestion support
verbatim_quote: DB2 provides significant support for complex data types, such as 'character' and 'double-byte character' data types (for internationalization), and large (up to 2 gigabytes) data types.

subject: DB2 Relational Extenders
object_value: text, imaging, audio, video server extenders; fingerprint analysis; SQL querying of image content by color, shape, pattern
verbatim_quote: Upcoming are bundles of triggers, user-defined data types, and user-defined functions for particular data types called Relational Database Extenders (e.g., a text server, imaging server, audio server, and video server).

subject: Aberdeen Group
object_value: text Extender includes information-retrieval technology; ability to detect user query patterns can improve data mining efficiency significantly
verbatim_quote: Aberdeen believes that the ability to detect and adjust to patterns of user queries can improve the efficiency and effectiveness of data mining on large text databases significantly.

subject: OS/2 Warp
object_value: IBM estimates over 10 million OS/2 users
verbatim_quote: IBM estimates over 10 million users of OS/2.

subject: Lotus Notes
object_value: upcoming incorporation of Lotus Notes into IBM product set will give DB2 close integration with leading groupware solution
verbatim_quote: The upcoming incorporation of Lotus Notes into IBM's product set should give DB2 especially close integration with today's leading groupware solution.

subject: NotesPump
object_value: provides replication support between Notes and DB2 via ODBC interface
verbatim_quote: Lotus' newly announced NotesPump provides replication support between Notes and DB2 via an ODBC interface.

subject: DB2
object_value: mainframe/AS/400 connectivity to open servers; large-scale decision-support/data-mart; OLTP on open systems as application servers
verbatim_quote: DB2 is especially effective where users with mainframe or midrange-AS/400 platforms wish to connect them to new open-server-hardware-based solutions, in large-scale decision-support situations as data marts interoperating with data warehouses on high-end and MPP platforms

subject: data-mart/data-warehouse
object_value: combinations of data marts on DB2 with data warehouses on RS/6000 SP increasingly attractive to avoid massive central data warehouse reviews
verbatim_quote: Such combinations are increasingly attractive to end users seeking to avoid the massive review of the enterprise's data involved in implementation of a single, central data warehouse.

subject: IBM
object_value: dedicated to being open client-server vendor; aims to incorporate best optimization technology and industrial-strength RDBMS functions plus object extensions
verbatim_quote: IBM states that it 'is dedicated to being an open, client-server vendor capable of providing industry-leading client/server solutions to customers'.

subject: IBM DB2
object_value: focusing DB2 on small office, home office market as single-user or unconnected-LAN RDBMS
verbatim_quote: IBM is also focusing DB2 on the SOHO (small office, home office) market as a single-user or unconnected-LAN RDBMS.

subject: IBM
object_value: IBM overall 1994 revenues were $64.0 billion, a 2% increase from 1993
verbatim_quote: IBM's overall 1994 revenues were $64.0 billion, a 2 % increase from 1993, with $3 billion in net profit.

subject: IBM
object_value: IBM 1994 net profit was $3 billion
verbatim_quote: IBM's overall 1994 revenues were $64.0 billion, a 2 % increase from 1993, with $3 billion in net profit.

subject: IBM software
object_value: IBM estimates software revenues were $11.3 billion in 1994, more than twice the largest software company
verbatim_quote: IBM estimates that its software revenues were $11.3 billion in 1994, which would make IBM's software revenues more than twice as large as those of the largest software company.

subject: DB2
object_value: DB2 is strong competitor in DORS market with high scalability, distributed-database, data-warehouse, open replication, admin tools; especially attractive on departmental/divisional IBM-hardware systems
verbatim_quote: DB2 is a strong competitor in the DORS market, with a robust solution and the ability to add other IBM products to achieve high scalability, distributed-database and data-warehouse support, open replication and administrative tools

subject: IBM
object_value: must fold ancillary products into DB2 bundle; upgrade client-server dev toolset; continue scalability advances; sell aggressively on non-IBM platforms; improve VAR marketing
verbatim_quote: The keys to DB2's success in the long term are its ability to continue to fold its ancillary products into an overall DB2 'bundle', upgrade its client-server development toolset capabilities, continue to move ahead rapidly in scalability technology

subject: Aberdeen Group
object_value: IS buyers seeking RDBMS-technology with legacy integration should closely examine new DB2
verbatim_quote: IS buyers seeking to have their RDBMS-technology cake, and leverage it too, should take a close look at the new DB2.

subject: IBM DB2
object_value: replication technology for data-warehousing; ability to incorporate mainframe and AS/400 in enterprise-scale RDBMS
verbatim_quote: IBM's major strengths in this competition are its replication technology, which supports IBM's data-warehousing efforts, and its ability to incorporate the mainframe and AS/400 in an enterprise-scale RDBMS.

subject: Aberdeen DORS Buying Guide
object_value: scalability; distributed-database support; flexibility; administrative support and robustness
verbatim_quote: Aberdeen's Distributed, Open RDBMS (DORS) Buying Guide finds that the following capabilities are key to IS' effective use of DORSs to create distributed, open production systems: scalability, distributed-database support, flexibility, and administrative support and robustness.

subject: DB2 v2
object_value: multithreading for SMP scalability, two-phase commit, large/complex data types (video, audio, text, imaging)
verbatim_quote: DB2 common server version 2 adds not only multithreading support for SMP scalability and two-phase commit, but also a wealth of other features...In version 2, IBM is extending this focus on details to include large and complex data types, with extensive support for video, audio, text, and imaging.
